item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, I like the idea, and the concept of these creatures miraculously surviving extinction events. The testing logs did feel a bit run-of-the-mill, but they weren't badly done.

I do wish that the image was at the top. If you do describe it so well, why not have a visual in your head of the creature while you read the article? Just a thought.

I think what I wanted the most is an extension/expansion of the current logs at the end of the article. It felt a bit short, and I didn't feel as if it hit the marks I wanted it to. (Length and emotion are the two big ones)

Imagine being trapped in what is essentially a death box surrounded by something you can't see. That's terrifying. If possible, maybe expand the logs to give a harder punch?

I will say, you did a pretty good job at terminology and keeping tone consistent through the whole thing.

No-vote for now, may review for later.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Site Crit | Feedback
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the crit! I didn't think the tests would be at fault, but thanks for pointing that out ^^ I didn't want the article to be too short so i came up with tests i felt were interesting, but each their own as its said.

item.title: Junior Staff Site Crit | Feedback
item.content:encoded:
Hey there! I noticed this has been out for a bit and hasn’t received much attention. I think one pig reason that this article might have turned people away is due to the multiple of test log types. There are many different types which after the first few entries of each, become repetitive and dull. I think you stretched the article out far too long, and lost your readers’ attention.

I suggest maybe getting more feedback to help you out, and checking out some guides.

Good luck!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Source of the image is right here.
I hope you enjoyed the article and thanks for reading it ^^
